enrichment.short_description: CurrencyFreaks is a developer-focused REST API that delivers real-time and historical exchange rate data for over 1,000 currencies, including fiat, precious metals, and cryptocurrencies. It is built and maintained by JFreaks and is aimed at developers who need a lightweight, SSL-secured JSON or XML feed to power currency conversion in apps, e-commerce sites, and financial dashboards. The service is bootstrapped and self-funded rather than venture backed.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium subscription with tiered monthly request limits
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; paid plans start at 9.99 USD per month
enrichment.seo_title: CurrencyFreaks Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CurrencyFreaks offers real-time and historical exchange rate data for 1,000+ currencies via a simple REST API. See 2026 pricing, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: CurrencyFreaks is a currency exchange rate API built for software developers who need to add live and historical foreign exchange, precious metal, and cryptocurrency pricing to their applications. The product is operated by JFreaks, a small, bootstrapped, customer-funded company headquartered in Lahore, Pakistan, that first shipped CurrencyFreaks publicly in May 2018 after conceiving the idea in late 2017. As of the most recent public data, the company remains a lean team of roughly 1 to 10 employees and has not raised outside venture funding, positioning itself as a low-cost, high-value alternative to larger incumbents such as Open Exchange Rates, XE, and Currencylayer. The core product is a single REST endpoint pattern that supports live rates, historical rates dating back to 1984, currency conversion, time-series and fluctuation data, and IP-to-currency detection, all returned in either JSON or XML. A single API call can return exchange data for all supported currencies at once or be filtered to specific base and target currencies, and typical response times are cited at under 200 milliseconds. CurrencyFreaks supports coverage for 1,026 total currency codes, comprising 166 to 174 fiat currencies (sources vary slightly by date), 4 precious metals, and several hundred cryptocurrencies, making it one of the broader currency data sets available at this price point. Every plan, including the free tier, is delivered over SSL-encrypted connections. The free plan permits 1,000 API calls per month with daily-updated rates and requires no credit card to sign up, which makes it popular for prototyping, student projects, and small internal tools. Paid plans scale by request volume and update frequency: the Starter plan (9.99 USD per month) provides 15,000 requests with hourly-refreshed rates and access to all base currencies; the Growth plan (49.99 USD per month) provides 150,000 requests with rates refreshed every 10 minutes, IP-to-currency lookups, and premium support; the Professional plan (99.99 USD per month) provides 550,000 requests with 60-second rate updates and access to time-series and fluctuation endpoints; and a custom Enterprise plan offers 60-second updates plus access to every endpoint for high-volume use cases. Typical use cases include e-commerce checkout localization, fintech and accounting dashboards, travel and booking platforms displaying multi-currency pricing, and internal reporting tools that need a dependable historical rate archive. Because CurrencyFreaks is a narrowly scoped utility API rather than a full platform, it is best evaluated against direct competitors like Currencylayer, CurrencyBeacon (formerly CurrencyScoop), Open Exchange Rates, Fixer, and ExchangeRate-API on the axes of price per request, update frequency, historical data depth, and currency coverage.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is CurrencyFreaks
paragraphs: CurrencyFreaks is a currency exchange rate API designed for developers who need to embed live or historical foreign exchange data into websites, mobile apps, or backend financial systems. It exposes a single, consistent REST endpoint style that can return live rates, historical rates, converted amounts, and time-series data depending on the query parameters supplied.

The service is built and operated by JFreaks, a small independent software company. Unlike some competitors that are owned by larger data or API conglomerates, CurrencyFreaks has remained self-funded and bootstrapped since its 2018 launch, which the company positions as a reason it can offer aggressive pricing relative to larger incumbents.

long_form_blocks.heading: Data coverage and update frequency
paragraphs: CurrencyFreaks covers 1,026 currency codes in total, spanning roughly 166 to 174 fiat currencies, 4 precious metals, and several hundred cryptocurrencies. Historical data is available going back to 1984 on applicable plans, which is deeper than many competing free-tier APIs.

Update frequency is tied to the subscription tier: free and entry paid plans refresh hourly or daily, while higher tiers refresh every 10 minutes, and the top commercial tiers refresh every 60 seconds for near-real-time accuracy.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who should use CurrencyFreaks
paragraphs: CurrencyFreaks fits teams that want a straightforward, affordably priced exchange rate feed without committing to an enterprise data vendor. It is a common choice for indie developers, small e-commerce shops, and startups building multi-currency pricing or invoicing features.

Larger organizations with strict SLA, compliance, or uptime requirements may still want to evaluate CurrencyFreaks alongside larger providers before committing, since the company is small and does not publish formal enterprise SLAs on its marketing pages.

enrichment.short_description: Currencylayer is a REST API that provides real-time and historical exchange rate data for 168 world currencies plus precious metals, delivered in JSON format. It is a product of apilayer, an API marketplace company founded in Vienna, Austria in 2015 that was itself acquired by Idera Inc. in January 2021. Currencylayer targets developers building currency conversion into commercial and internal applications.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium subscription with tiered monthly request limits and update-frequency gating
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; paid plans start at 13.99 USD per month
enrichment.seo_title: Currencylayer Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Currencylayer delivers real-time and historical exchange rates for 168 currencies via REST API. Compare 2026 pricing plans, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Currencylayer is a currency exchange rate API that supplies real-time and historical foreign exchange data for 168 world currencies plus precious metals, formatted as portable JSON compatible with virtually any programming language or framework. It was founded in 2015 by brothers Julian and Paul Zehetmayr in Vienna, Austria, as one of the flagship products of apilayer, a company that builds and sells a portfolio of developer-facing data APIs. In January 2021, apilayer itself was acquired by Idera, Inc., a portfolio company known for owning a range of developer and database tools, which means Currencylayer today operates as a product line inside a larger, private-equity-backed software group rather than as a standalone startup. The API sources spot exchange rate data from multiple major forex data providers, validating and processing the feed before delivery, with refresh frequency gated by subscription tier: the free plan updates once daily, the Basic plan updates hourly, the Professional plan updates every 10 minutes, and the top Business Plus plan updates within a 60-second market window. Currencylayer's endpoints include live rates, historical rates reaching back roughly 19 years (to 1999 in the company's own documentation), currency conversion, time-frame queries between two dates, and currency-change or fluctuation queries, all keyed to a default USD base on lower tiers with more flexible base currency support on paid tiers. Pricing as of 2026 is structured as: a free tier for evaluation and light use; a Basic plan at 13.99 USD per month (or 161.99 USD per year, roughly a 10 percent discount) offering commercial usage rights and up to 10,000 requests per month; a Professional plan at 52.99 USD per month (or 629.99 USD per year, about 12.5 percent off) with premium features and up to 100,000 monthly requests; and a Business Plus plan at 84.99 USD per month (or 1,019.99 USD per year, about 15 percent off) unlocking every feature and up to 500,000 monthly requests. Currencylayer is also distributed through the RapidAPI marketplace as an alternative purchasing channel. Because it is one of the older, more established players in the currency-API category and carries the apilayer and Idera brand behind it, Currencylayer is often positioned by third parties as a more enterprise-credible option than newer bootstrapped alternatives, though it is priced somewhat higher at comparable usage tiers than challengers such as CurrencyFreaks. Typical customers are software teams building e-commerce, fintech, travel, ERP, or accounting products that require dependable multi-currency conversion, historical rate auditing, or fluctuation reporting. Buyers evaluating Currencylayer in 2026 should weigh its longer track record and corporate backing against its higher per-tier pricing relative to some newer entrants in the same space.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Currencylayer
paragraphs: Currencylayer is a REST API delivering real-time and historical foreign exchange rate data for 168 world currencies plus select precious metals. It is one of the original products offered by apilayer, an Austrian API marketplace company, and has been operating since 2015.

The API returns data in JSON format designed to be compatible with any modern application stack, and it is commonly used as a drop-in data source for currency conversion features in commercial software.

long_form_blocks.heading: Ownership and company background
paragraphs: Currencylayer was founded by Julian and Paul Zehetmayr as part of apilayer, headquartered in Vienna, Austria. In January 2021, apilayer was acquired by Idera, Inc., a company that owns a broad portfolio of developer and database tooling brands.

This means Currencylayer is no longer an independent startup product but rather a business unit inside a larger software holding group, which can offer buyers more stability but also means product direction is set at the portfolio level.

long_form_blocks.heading: How Currencylayer pricing tiers work
paragraphs: Currencylayer pricing scales along two axes: monthly request volume and rate refresh frequency. The free plan is limited to daily-updated rates, while paid tiers unlock progressively faster refresh windows down to 60 seconds on the top Business Plus plan.
Annual billing offers a discount of roughly 10 to 15 percent depending on the tier, which can meaningfully reduce cost for teams with predictable, ongoing usage.

enrichment.short_description: CurrencyScoop was a REST API offering real-time and historical exchange rate data for 168 world currencies in JSON format. As of 2026, the currencyscoop.com domain redirects to CurrencyBeacon, indicating the product has been rebranded and consolidated under the CurrencyBeacon name, which now serves as the active successor product for the same underlying currency data service.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium subscription with tiered monthly request limits (now operated under the CurrencyBeacon brand)
enrichment.starting_price: Free developer sandbox available; paid plans start at approximately 10 USD per month under the successor CurrencyBeacon brand
enrichment.seo_title: CurrencyScoop Review 2026: Status, Pricing and CurrencyBeacon Rebrand
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CurrencyScoop offered real-time and historical exchange rate data via REST API. Learn about its 2026 status, rebrand to CurrencyBeacon, and pricing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: CurrencyScoop was positioned as a developer-friendly REST API delivering real-time and historical currency exchange rate data for 168 world currencies, returned in a universally usable JSON format compatible with any programming language or application. The service sourced spot exchange rate data from multiple major forex data providers, central banks, and commercial vendors, validating and processing it before delivery, and historically offered a free trial tier providing up to 5,000 requests per day after email verification, with no credit card required to start. As of 2026, the official currencyscoop.com domain no longer hosts the original CurrencyScoop product directly; instead it performs a permanent redirect to currencybeacon.com, a currency data API business that describes itself as having started over a decade ago as a currency log before evolving into its current form, and that now says it serves more than 100,000 businesses and developers. This redirect pattern, combined with third-party integration platforms (such as Pipedream, Composio, and Relevance AI) that still reference 'CurrencyScoop' as an app name while pointing to what is functionally the same currency-data service, strongly suggests that CurrencyScoop was rebranded or consolidated into CurrencyBeacon rather than shut down outright, though the exact corporate mechanics of that transition (acquisition, internal rebrand, or asset transfer) are not clearly documented in public sources. Because of this, anyone researching 'CurrencyScoop' in 2026 should treat CurrencyBeacon as the current, actively maintained product to evaluate for real usage. Under the CurrencyBeacon brand, the currency data API is offered on a tiered subscription model: a free Developer Sandbox plan providing 5,000 requests with hourly rate updates; a Basic plan at 10 USD per month (or 100 USD per year) providing 50,000 requests with rates refreshed every 15 minutes; a Startup plan at 99 USD per month (or 900 USD per year) providing 500,000 requests with rates refreshed every minute; and a Professional plan at 599 USD per month (or 6,500 USD per year) providing 2,000,000 requests with real-time rate updates. The product also exposes an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server so AI agents and LLM-based tools can query exchange rates directly, and REST API calls share the same usage quota as MCP tool calls. Because the original CurrencyScoop brand, founding details, headquarters, and team size are not clearly documented in current public sources following the transition to CurrencyBeacon, those specific facts should be treated as unconfirmed rather than assumed. Typical use cases for this category of API, both under the old CurrencyScoop name and its CurrencyBeacon successor, include e-commerce currency conversion, fintech reporting, travel price localization, and increasingly, giving AI agents a live data source for currency-related questions via the MCP integration.

long_form_blocks.heading: What CurrencyScoop was
paragraphs: CurrencyScoop was marketed as a simple REST API delivering real-time and historical exchange rates for 168 world currencies, aimed at developers who needed currency pair data and time-series information in JSON format.
It offered a free trial tier with up to 5,000 requests per day and was also distributed through the RapidAPI marketplace as an alternative access channel.

long_form_blocks.heading: The transition to CurrencyBeacon
paragraphs: As of 2026, visiting currencyscoop.com redirects visitors to currencybeacon.com, a currency exchange rate API with a similar feature set, real-time and historical fiat and cryptocurrency conversion, and a comparable REST API design.

This pattern, along with references to CurrencyScoop persisting inside various third-party integration directories, indicates a rebrand or brand consolidation rather than a simple shutdown, though the precise corporate details of the transition are not clearly published.

long_form_blocks.heading: What to evaluate today
paragraphs: Buyers researching CurrencyScoop in 2026 should evaluate CurrencyBeacon directly, since it appears to be the actively maintained successor serving the same underlying use case.

CurrencyBeacon's published pricing spans a free Developer Sandbox up to a 599 USD per month Professional plan with real-time updates and 2,000,000 monthly requests, plus a Model Context Protocol server for AI agent integrations.

Data sourced from multiple forex providers and central banks
Successor product (CurrencyBeacon) is actively maintained with modern additions like MCP support
Straightforward JSON output compatible with most tech stacks
cons: The original CurrencyScoop brand is no longer directly accessible as a standalone product
Company founding year, headquarters, and team size are not clearly documented publicly
Buyers must now research and trust the successor CurrencyBeacon brand instead
Potential confusion for existing integrations still labeled CurrencyScoop in third-party platforms
Lack of clear public communication about the rebrand transition

faqs.question: Is CurrencyScoop still active in 2026
faqs.answer: The original currencyscoop.com domain now redirects to CurrencyBeacon, indicating the product was rebranded or consolidated rather than remaining active under its original name.

faqs.question: What is CurrencyBeacon's relationship to CurrencyScoop
faqs.answer: CurrencyBeacon appears to be the active successor product; the CurrencyScoop domain redirects there and several integration platforms still reference the CurrencyScoop name for what is now the CurrencyBeacon service.

faqs.question: What currencies did CurrencyScoop support
faqs.answer: It supported real-time and historical exchange rate data for 168 world currencies.

faqs.question: Did CurrencyScoop have a free plan
faqs.answer: It offered a free trial with up to 5,000 requests per day, requiring only email verification.

faqs.question: How much does the successor CurrencyBeacon service cost
faqs.answer: Plans range from a free Developer Sandbox to 599 USD per month for the Professional tier with real-time updates and 2,000,000 monthly requests.

faqs.question: Can AI agents query the successor product directly
faqs.answer: Yes, CurrencyBeacon exposes a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server so AI agents and LLM tools can query exchange rates directly.

faqs.question: Was CurrencyScoop available on RapidAPI
faqs.answer: Yes, it was listed on the RapidAPI marketplace as an alternative access channel for the same currency data.

faqs.question: Should I integrate with CurrencyScoop or CurrencyBeacon today
faqs.answer: Since currencyscoop.com now redirects to CurrencyBeacon, new integrations should target CurrencyBeacon directly.

enrichment.short_description: Customer Lobby is a marketing and reputation platform for home and field service businesses, such as HVAC, plumbing, electrical, and carpet cleaning companies, that ties marketing spend to sales results and automates personalized customer outreach. Founded in 2007 by Ted Paff in the San Francisco Bay Area, it was later acquired by EverCommerce and today operates as part of EverCommerce's EverPro division of home-service software brands.
enrichment.starting_price: Plans have historically started around 299 USD per month for the Pro Marketing tier
enrichment.company_size: Not publicly disclosed as a standalone entity (operates as a brand within EverCommerce, a publicly traded company)
enrichment.headquarters: Denver, Colorado, United States (as an EverCommerce/EverPro brand; originally founded in the Oakland/Berkeley, California area)
enrichment.seo_title: Customer Lobby Review 2026: Pricing, Features, EverCommerce Status
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Customer Lobby helps home service businesses generate repeat business. See 2026 pricing, features, EverCommerce ownership, and pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Customer Lobby is a marketing automation and customer retention platform built specifically for local and home-service businesses, including HVAC, plumbing, electrical, carpet cleaning, home improvement, and auto service companies. It was founded in 2007 by Ted Paff, who self-financed and built the company before eventually selling it. As of 2026, Customer Lobby operates as a brand within EverCommerce (NASDAQ: EVCM), a publicly traded, multi-brand vertical software company, specifically under EverCommerce's EverPro division, which bundles trade-specific software for home and field service professionals and is described as trusted by more than 350,000 home and field service businesses across the EverPro portfolio. This means Customer Lobby is no longer an independently operated startup; its product roadmap, support infrastructure, and go-to-market motion are aligned with EverCommerce's broader home-services software strategy, which also includes sibling EverPro brands. The core Customer Lobby product connects to a business's existing invoicing or job-management system and uses that transaction data to trigger personalized, multi-channel marketing messages, print mailers, email, and other outreach aimed at both past customers (to drive repeat business) and prospective customers identified through predictive targeting. The platform emphasizes marketing accountability: it explicitly ties marketing dollars spent to sales results generated, which is a differentiator against generic email marketing tools that do not close the loop back to revenue. Key functional areas include Prospect Marketing, which uses predictive modeling to identify likely-to-convert local prospects, and Customer Marketing, which automates retention campaigns to existing customers, largely running with low day-to-day maintenance once configured. Historical pricing data shows plans starting around 299 USD per month for a Pro Marketing tier, though EverCommerce and EverPro do not always publish transparent, self-serve pricing, and prospective buyers are typically routed through a sales demo request rather than a public price list, which is common in the home-services SaaS category (a sibling product, LobbyCentral, is also part of the same company's portfolio and has separate published pricing). Customer Lobby's target buyer is an owner or marketing manager at a small-to-midsize home or field service business who wants to reduce reliance on expensive paid lead generation channels and instead extract more repeat and referral revenue from an existing customer base. Given the EverCommerce ownership structure, buyers should expect the product to be positioned and cross-sold alongside other EverPro tools (such as field service management, invoicing, and review-generation software) rather than sold purely as a standalone reputation-management point solution.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Customer Lobby
paragraphs: Customer Lobby is a marketing automation platform purpose-built for local and home-service businesses. It connects to a company's existing invoicing or job data to trigger targeted, multi-channel marketing campaigns aimed at both retaining existing customers and acquiring new prospects.

The platform is explicitly built around the idea of tying marketing spend to measurable sales outcomes, which is intended to help owner-operators in trades like HVAC, plumbing, and carpet cleaning justify and optimize their marketing budgets.

long_form_blocks.heading: Ownership: EverCommerce and EverPro
paragraphs: Customer Lobby was founded in 2007 by Ted Paff and was later acquired by EverCommerce, a publicly traded (NASDAQ: EVCM) vertical software company that owns a large portfolio of industry-specific SaaS brands.

Within EverCommerce, Customer Lobby sits under the EverPro division, which focuses specifically on home and field service software, alongside other trade-specific tools serving the same customer base.

long_form_blocks.heading: Core capabilities
paragraphs: The platform's two primary capabilities are Prospect Marketing, which uses predictive targeting to identify qualified new leads in a business's service area, and Customer Marketing, which automates personalized retention campaigns to the existing customer base.

Because campaigns are designed to run with minimal ongoing maintenance after initial setup, the product is positioned as a low-effort way for busy trade business owners to keep marketing running consistently.

faqs.question: Who is Customer Lobby designed for
faqs.answer: It is designed for local and home-service businesses such as HVAC, plumbing, electrical, carpet cleaning, home improvement, and auto service companies.

faqs.question: Does Customer Lobby integrate with invoicing systems
faqs.answer: Yes, it connects to a business's existing invoicing or job-management data to trigger relevant marketing campaigns.

faqs.question: Is Customer Lobby still an independent company
faqs.answer: No, it was acquired by EverCommerce and now operates as a brand within EverCommerce's EverPro division rather than as an independent company.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Prospect Marketing and Customer Marketing in Customer Lobby
faqs.answer: Prospect Marketing uses predictive targeting to find new qualified leads, while Customer Marketing automates retention campaigns to a business's existing customers.

enrichment.short_description: CustomJS is a cloud platform that lets developers and no-code builders run custom JavaScript and Node.js functions as serverless API endpoints, alongside tools for PDF generation, screenshots, form building, web scraping, and email webhooks. It is operated by TechnologyCircle GmbH, headquartered in Hamburg, Germany, and integrates directly with automation platforms like Make, n8n, Zapier, and Power Automate as well as raw API access.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium subscription with tiered daily request limits
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan available; paid plans start at 9 USD per month
enrichment.seo_title: CustomJS Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Use Cases
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CustomJS runs custom JavaScript as serverless endpoints for PDFs, forms, scraping and more. See 2026 pricing plans, features, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: CustomJS is a cloud execution platform that allows developers and no-code automation builders to run custom JavaScript and Node.js code as hosted, callable API endpoints without managing servers. It is built and operated by TechnologyCircle GmbH, a company headquartered in Hamburg, Germany. Rather than positioning itself purely as a generic serverless function host, CustomJS bundles several purpose-built capabilities around its JavaScript execution engine: full NPM package support for Node.js code, HTML-to-PDF document generation, website screenshot capture, static page and form hosting, a drag-and-drop form builder, headless forms for embedding in other sites, a mail hook feature that forwards incoming email to a webhook, a web scraper API for extracting data from pages, and a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server so AI agents can call CustomJS functions as tools. The platform is explicitly designed to plug the gaps that no-code automation tools leave behind: it integrates directly with Make (formerly Integromat), n8n, Microsoft Power Automate, and Zapier, letting non-developers or citcitzen-developer teams drop in a snippet of custom JavaScript logic in the middle of an otherwise visual automation workflow, in addition to being callable via a direct REST API for traditional developers. Pricing is structured around a small number of straightforward monthly tiers, each billed either monthly or annually (with roughly 25 percent savings on annual billing). The Free plan allows 20 requests per day, 3 forms with up to 500 monthly responses, 1 mail hook with up to 10 daily incoming emails, and 5 hosted pages, with email-only support and no credit card required to start. The Pro plan, at 9 USD per month, raises limits to 100 requests per day, 9 forms with 2,000 monthly responses, 3 mail hooks with 50 daily incoming emails, 15 hosted pages, and 1 custom domain. The Ultra plan, at 29 USD per month, further raises limits to 500 requests per day, 29 forms with 6,000 monthly responses, 19 mail hooks with 250 daily incoming emails, 40 hosted pages, and adds direct JavaScript coding support from the CustomJS team. The Mega plan, at 99 USD per month, is the top published tier, offering 5,000 requests per day, 59 forms with 30,000 monthly responses, 49 mail hooks with 1,000 daily incoming emails, 100 hosted pages, and full support including scheduled video call assistance. All paid plans remove watermarks from generated PDFs, which is a common consideration for teams generating client-facing documents. Because CustomJS sits at the intersection of a serverless function runtime and a no-code automation utility belt, its most common users are automation-focused teams (agencies and internal ops teams building Make or n8n workflows), solo developers who need quick custom logic without standing up their own cloud infrastructure, and AI/agent builders who want a way to give an LLM agent the ability to execute arbitrary JavaScript, generate a PDF, or scrape a webpage as a callable tool.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is CustomJS
paragraphs: CustomJS is a cloud platform for running custom JavaScript and Node.js code as hosted API endpoints, without the user needing to provision or manage any server infrastructure. Full NPM package support means most standard Node.js code can run largely unmodified.

Beyond raw code execution, the platform bundles several adjacent utilities including PDF generation from HTML, website screenshot capture, form building and hosting, static page hosting, an email-to-webhook mail hook, and a web scraping API, all accessible from the same account and API surface.

long_form_blocks.heading: Integrations with no-code automation tools
paragraphs: A core part of CustomJS's positioning is direct integration with popular no-code and automation platforms, including Make, n8n, Microsoft Power Automate, and Zapier. This lets non-developers insert a piece of custom JavaScript logic into an otherwise visual, drag-and-drop automation workflow.

CustomJS also exposes a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, allowing AI agents built on frameworks that support MCP to call CustomJS capabilities, such as PDF generation or code execution, as callable tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: How CustomJS pricing is structured
paragraphs: CustomJS uses a four-tier model from Free up to Mega, with limits scaling primarily along daily request volume, number of forms and mail hooks allowed, and monthly form response quotas. Annual billing offers roughly 25 percent savings versus paying monthly.

Higher tiers add qualitative benefits beyond raw quota increases, such as direct JavaScript coding support on the Ultra plan and dedicated video call support on the Mega plan, which is somewhat unusual for a product at this price point.

faqs.question: What is CustomJS used for
faqs.answer: It is used to run custom JavaScript or Node.js code as serverless API endpoints, and to generate PDFs, capture screenshots, build forms, and scrape web pages.

faqs.question: Does CustomJS have a free plan
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan allows 20 requests per day, 3 forms, and 5 hosted pages with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does CustomJS cost
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at 9 USD per month for the Pro tier, up to 99 USD per month for the Mega tier.

faqs.question: Who operates CustomJS
faqs.answer: CustomJS is operated by TechnologyCircle GmbH, headquartered in Hamburg, Germany.

faqs.question: Does CustomJS integrate with Zapier or Make
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates directly with Make, n8n, Microsoft Power Automate, and Zapier for use inside no-code automation workflows.

faqs.question: Can AI agents use CustomJS
faqs.answer: Yes, CustomJS exposes a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server so AI agents can call its functions, such as PDF generation or code execution, as tools.

faqs.question: Does CustomJS support NPM packages
faqs.answer: Yes, its JavaScript execution environment offers full NPM support for Node.js code.

faqs.question: Do paid CustomJS plans remove PDF watermarks
faqs.answer: Yes, all paid plans include white-label PDF generation without watermarks.

enrichment.short_description: CyberCube is a cyber risk analytics platform that provides insurers, reinsurers, and brokers with data-driven tools to quantify, price, and manage cyber insurance risk and accumulation exposure. Founded in 2015 and originally incubated within Symantec before spinning out as a standalone company, CyberCube is headquartered in San Francisco, California, and its clients include a large majority of the global cyber (re)insurance market by premium.
enrichment.pricing_model: Enterprise subscription/licensing, custom quoted per client
enrichment.seo_title: CyberCube Review 2026: Cyber Risk Analytics for Insurers
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CyberCube provides cyber risk analytics for insurers and reinsurers. Learn about its 2026 pricing model, features, clients, and use cases.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: CyberCube is a cyber risk analytics and modeling company built specifically to serve the insurance industry, providing data and software tools that help insurers, reinsurers, brokers, and managing general agents quantify, price, and manage exposure to cyber risk. The platform was originally established in 2015 inside Symantec, the cybersecurity company, before being spun out to operate as a standalone, insurance-focused analytics business. CyberCube is headquartered in San Francisco, California, and as of 2026 employs somewhere in the range of roughly 120 to 135 people according to various third-party data providers, operating across multiple continents. The company has taken on significant institutional investment to fund its growth: in 2025, private equity firm Spectrum Equity joined CyberCube's existing financial backers as a new cornerstone investor in a financing round exceeding 180 million USD, aimed at accelerating the company's global expansion. CyberCube's client base is notably concentrated at the top of the insurance market; the company states that its analytics are used by more than two-thirds of the global cyber (re)insurance market measured by premium volume, with publicly referenced clients and partners including major names such as Munich Re, Chubb, Guy Carpenter, and Aon. The core of CyberCube's offering is software that models aggregation risk (the risk that a single cyber event, such as a major cloud outage or a widespread ransomware campaign, could trigger correlated losses across many policyholders at once), individual account underwriting risk, and portfolio-level accumulation risk, giving underwriters and portfolio managers data-driven insight for pricing decisions and capital allocation. One named product within its suite, Account Manager, is described as purpose-built for insurance underwriter workflows, surfacing predictive insights that help underwriters make more data-driven risk selection and pricing decisions at the individual account level; a separate Portfolio Manager product addresses aggregation and accumulation risk across an insurer's entire book of cyber business. Because CyberCube sells to large enterprise insurance and reinsurance organizations rather than individual consumers or small businesses, it does not publish self-serve or tiered public pricing; engagements are quoted and negotiated directly with each client based on scope, data needs, and deployment scale, which is standard practice in the specialty insurance analytics category. CyberCube competes in a niche but consequential category, since accurately modeling cyber catastrophe risk is considered one of the harder open problems in the insurance industry given the correlated, fast-evolving, and adversarial nature of cyber threats compared to more traditional catastrophe perils like hurricanes or earthquakes. Typical buyers are underwriting teams at primary insurers, reinsurance treaty and portfolio managers, insurance brokers advising clients on cyber coverage placement, and increasingly, insurance-linked securities (ILS) investors who need a standardized way to assess cyber catastrophe bond and reinsurance risk.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is CyberCube
paragraphs: CyberCube is a cyber risk analytics company that builds software and data models specifically for the insurance industry, helping insurers, reinsurers, brokers, and MGAs understand, price, and manage exposure to cyber risk.
The platform originated in 2015 inside Symantec before spinning off as an independent company focused exclusively on the insurance vertical, and it is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

long_form_blocks.heading: Core products: underwriting and portfolio risk
paragraphs: CyberCube's Account Manager product is designed for individual underwriting workflows, surfacing predictive risk insights to help underwriters make more informed pricing and selection decisions on specific accounts.

Its Portfolio Manager product addresses accumulation risk at the book level, helping insurers and reinsurers understand how a single large-scale cyber event could generate correlated losses across many policies simultaneously.

long_form_blocks.heading: Market position and funding
paragraphs: CyberCube states that its analytics are used by more than two-thirds of the global cyber (re)insurance market by premium, with clients including major reinsurers, brokers, and insurers such as Munich Re, Chubb, Guy Carpenter, and Aon.

In 2025, Spectrum Equity joined CyberCube's roster of financial backers as a new cornerstone investor in a financing round exceeding 180 million USD, signaling continued institutional confidence in the cyber risk analytics category.

faqs.answer: CyberCube is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

faqs.answer: No, CyberCube uses an enterprise sales model with custom quoted pricing rather than published self-serve pricing.

faqs.answer: It helps insurers and reinsurers manage accumulation risk, assessing how a single cyber event could cause correlated losses across an entire portfolio.

enrichment.short_description: CyberGhost is a consumer VPN service that encrypts internet traffic, masks IP addresses, and unblocks geo-restricted streaming content across thousands of servers worldwide. Founded in Romania in 2011, it was acquired in 2017 by Crossrider, which later rebranded as Kape Technologies, the same parent company that also owns ExpressVPN and Private Internet Access.
enrichment.pricing_model: Consumer subscription with tiered commitment lengths
enrichment.starting_price: Long-term plans as low as roughly 2 USD per month; month-to-month plan around 12.99 USD per month
enrichment.seo_title: CyberGhost VPN Review 2026: Pricing, Features, Kape Ownership
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CyberGhost VPN offers encrypted browsing and streaming unblocking. See 2026 pricing plans, features, Kape Technologies ownership, pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: CyberGhost is a well-known consumer virtual private network (VPN) service that encrypts a user's internet traffic and routes it through remote servers to mask their IP address, protect privacy on public networks, and unblock geographically restricted content such as international streaming catalogs. The company was founded in Romania in 2011 as an independent VPN provider and is headquartered in Bucharest, at Strada Dionisie Lupu 70. In 2017, CyberGhost was acquired by Crossrider, an Israeli-founded software company that subsequently rebranded itself as Kape Technologies. Kape Technologies has since consolidated a portfolio of consumer privacy brands under one roof, and today CyberGhost is a sibling brand to ExpressVPN and Private Internet Access (PIA), two other major VPN services also owned by Kape. This shared ownership means CyberGhost benefits from Kape's larger network infrastructure, security research resources, and corporate stability, though it also means CyberGhost competes internally for attention and investment against sister brands in the same company's portfolio. As of 2026, CyberGhost's team is reported at somewhere between roughly 70 and 103 employees depending on the data source, with development staff based across both Romania and Germany, the latter handling the bulk of the software engineering work. On the product side, CyberGhost offers thousands of servers across dozens of countries, applies strong encryption protocols, includes a no-logs privacy policy, and provides dedicated streaming-optimized servers designed to reliably access services like major international streaming platforms. It supports simultaneous connections across multiple devices per subscription, offers apps across major desktop, mobile, and router platforms, and includes standard VPN features such as a kill switch, split tunneling, and ad/tracker blocking on many plans. Pricing follows the standard VPN industry pattern of steep discounts for longer commitments: CyberGhost's most economical rate is achieved through its longest-term plan (historically around a 2-plus year commitment), which can bring the effective monthly cost down to roughly 2 USD when paid upfront in a single larger charge (commonly cited around 56.94 USD total for the discounted long-term period, often bundled with several months free); a 6-month plan runs at a higher effective monthly rate (historically cited around 6.99 USD per month, billed as a lump sum); and month-to-month billing is the most expensive option, historically priced around 12.99 USD per month. CyberGhost backs its plans with money-back guarantee windows, typically a shorter guarantee (around 14 days) on the monthly plan and a longer guarantee (around 45 days) on its 6-month and multi-year plans, which is longer than the industry-standard 30-day window offered by many competitors. Typical CyberGhost users are privacy-conscious consumers, travelers wanting to access home-country streaming content abroad, and users on public Wi-Fi looking for straightforward encryption without needing deep technical VPN configuration knowledge.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is CyberGhost
paragraphs: CyberGhost is a consumer VPN service that encrypts internet traffic and routes it through a large network of remote servers, protecting user privacy on public and private networks and enabling access to content that may be geographically restricted in the user's actual location.
It is one of the more recognizable consumer VPN brands globally, competing directly with services such as NordVPN, ExpressVPN, and Surfshark on price, server count, and streaming reliability.

long_form_blocks.heading: Ownership: Kape Technologies
paragraphs: CyberGhost was founded as an independent Romanian VPN company in 2011 before being acquired in 2017 by Crossrider, which later rebranded itself as Kape Technologies.

Kape Technologies also owns ExpressVPN and Private Internet Access, meaning CyberGhost operates as one of several sibling VPN brands within the same corporate parent, sharing some backend infrastructure and security resources across the portfolio.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing structure and money-back guarantees
paragraphs: Like most consumer VPNs, CyberGhost's pricing rewards longer commitments heavily: the lowest effective monthly rate is only available on its longest-term plan, while month-to-month billing costs several times more per month.

CyberGhost differentiates itself somewhat on its refund policy, offering a 45-day money-back guarantee on its 6-month and multi-year plans, longer than the 30-day standard common across much of the VPN industry, alongside a shorter 14-day guarantee on the monthly plan.

features.description: Provides apps for major desktop, mobile, and router platforms.

features.description: Shares corporate resources and security investment with sibling brands ExpressVPN and Private Internet Access.

Large server network with strong streaming unblocking performance
Extended 45-day money-back guarantee on longer plans
Backed by the resources and stability of Kape Technologies
Long operating history dating back to 2011
User-friendly apps suitable for less technical users
cons: Month-to-month pricing is significantly more expensive than long-term commitments

As with all VPNs, absolute no-logs claims cannot be independently verified by end users

faqs.question: Who owns CyberGhost VPN
faqs.answer: CyberGhost is owned by Kape Technologies, which acquired it in 2017 through its earlier entity Crossrider; Kape also owns ExpressVPN and Private Internet Access.

faqs.question: When was CyberGhost founded
faqs.answer: CyberGhost was founded in Romania in 2011.

faqs.question: How much does CyberGhost cost
faqs.answer: Pricing ranges from roughly 2 USD per month on the longest-term plan to around 12.99 USD per month for month-to-month billing.

faqs.question: Does CyberGhost offer a money-back guarantee
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers a 14-day guarantee on the monthly plan and a 45-day guarantee on its 6-month and multi-year plans.

faqs.question: Where is CyberGhost headquartered
faqs.answer: CyberGhost is headquartered in Bucharest, Romania.

faqs.question: Is CyberGhost good for streaming
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers dedicated streaming-optimized servers designed to reliably access major international streaming platforms.

faqs.question: How many devices can I use with one CyberGhost subscription
faqs.answer: CyberGhost supports simultaneous connections across multiple devices per subscription.

faqs.question: What other VPN brands does CyberGhost's parent company own
faqs.answer: Kape Technologies, CyberGhost's parent company, also owns ExpressVPN and Private Internet Access.

enrichment.short_description: Cypress is a popular open-source, JavaScript-based end-to-end and component testing framework for web applications, paired with a commercial companion service called Cypress Cloud for test recording, parallelization, and analytics. As of 2026, Cypress.io, Inc. remains an independently operated company headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia; despite integration partnerships with test-cloud providers such as Sauce Labs, there is no verified evidence that Cypress has been acquired by Sauce Labs or any other company.
enrichment.pricing_model: Open-source core framework, freemium SaaS for Cypress Cloud
enrichment.starting_price: Cypress the testing framework is free and open source; Cypress Cloud paid plans start at roughly 67 USD per month
enrichment.seo_title: Cypress Review 2026: Pricing, Features, Company Status Explained
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Cypress is a leading JavaScript end-to-end testing framework. See 2026 pricing, features, and the truth about its company and acquisition status.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Cypress is a widely used, open-source JavaScript testing framework for anything that runs in a browser, supporting end-to-end testing, component testing, and increasingly accessibility testing for modern web applications. The open-source Cypress Test Runner is free, released under the MIT License, and can be installed and used without any license fee, which has made it one of the most downloaded testing tools in the JavaScript ecosystem, with weekly npm downloads in the range of 6 to 6.5 million as of 2026 (though rival framework Playwright has grown faster over the same period, from roughly 8 million to 33 million weekly downloads). The company behind Cypress, Cypress.io, Inc., was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia. It raised a total of roughly 54.8 million USD across five funding rounds from investors including Bessemer Venture Partners, OpenView, and Tech Square Ventures, with its most recent disclosed round being a 40 million USD Series B in November 2020. As of 2026, Cypress.io, Inc. employs approximately 100 people spread across five continents and is reported to generate somewhere in the range of 25 to 60 million USD in annual revenue depending on the data source. Importantly, despite claims that circulate suggesting Cypress was acquired by Sauce Labs, independent research finds no verified evidence of any such acquisition: Sauce Labs' documented acquisition history (including Backtrace I/O, TestFairy, AutonomIQ, API Fortress, and Screener) does not include Cypress, and Sauce Labs' relationship with Cypress is a technical integration partnership, allowing Cypress tests to run on Sauce Labs' cloud infrastructure via the saucectl CLI, first announced around February 2021, not a corporate acquisition. A separate widely circulated claim that Cypress was 'joining John Deere' originates from a blog post published on April 1 (April Fools' Day) by a former Cypress team member and is explicitly satirical rather than a real corporate transaction. As of current public information, Cypress.io, Inc. remains an independently operated, venture-backed private company. Commercially, Cypress monetizes through Cypress Cloud, a companion SaaS dashboard for recording test runs, parallelizing test execution across machines, flake detection, and analytics. Cypress Cloud pricing in 2026 spans a free Starter tier with a limited monthly allotment of recorded test results, a Team tier priced around 67 USD per month supporting roughly 20 users with tens of thousands of monthly test results and around 10 parallel execution lanes, a Business tier priced around 267 USD per month supporting SSO and higher usage and parallelization limits, and a custom-quoted Enterprise tier for the largest organizations; annual contracts commonly carry negotiated discounts of roughly 10 to 30 percent versus list pricing. Core open-source features that make Cypress popular among developers include automatic waiting (eliminating manual sleeps or waits in test code), time-travel debugging that lets developers hover over each test command to see a snapshot of the application at that exact moment, network traffic stubbing and spying, fixtures for managing mock test data, and native component testing support for frameworks including React, Vue, and Angular. Typical users are front-end and full-stack engineering teams building automated regression suites for web applications, QA engineers integrating Cypress into continuous integration pipelines, and teams evaluating Cypress against alternatives such as Playwright, Selenium, and Puppeteer for their end-to-end testing strategy.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Cypress
paragraphs: Cypress is an open-source JavaScript testing framework designed for testing anything that runs in a browser, including full end-to-end user flows and isolated UI components for frameworks like React, Vue, and Angular.

The framework runs directly inside the browser alongside the application under test, which gives it native access to the DOM and network layer and enables features like automatic waiting and detailed time-travel debugging that are harder to achieve with browser-automation-based tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Company status: independent, not acquired by Sauce Labs
paragraphs: Cypress.io, Inc. was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia. As of 2026, it remains an independently operated, privately held, venture-backed company with roughly 100 employees, and there is no verified public record of it being acquired by Sauce Labs or any other company.

The confusion around a Sauce Labs acquisition likely stems from a genuine technical integration, Sauce Labs added official support for running Cypress tests on its cloud via the saucectl CLI around February 2021, which is a partnership, not a change of ownership. A separate viral claim about Cypress 'joining John Deere' traces back to an April Fools' Day blog post and is satire, not a real corporate event.

long_form_blocks.heading: Open-source framework versus Cypress Cloud
paragraphs: The core Cypress Test Runner is free and open source under the MIT License and can be used indefinitely without payment for local test authoring and execution.

Cypress Cloud is the company's commercial SaaS layer on top of the open-source tool, adding recorded test run history, parallelization across CI machines, automatic flake detection, and team collaboration analytics, monetized through the tiered pricing plans described above.

features.title: Automatic waiting
features.description: Cypress automatically waits for commands and assertions to resolve, removing the need for manual sleeps or waits in test code.

features.title: Time-travel debugging
features.description: Lets developers hover over each executed command to see a snapshot of the application state at that exact point in the test.

features.title: Native component testing
features.description: Mounts and tests individual UI components in isolation for frameworks including React, Vue, and Angular.

features.title: Network stubbing and spying
features.description: Provides tools to intercept, stub, and assert on network requests and responses during tests.

features.title: Real-time test execution view
features.description: Shows the application under test and executing commands side by side as tests run, with automatic re-runs on file save.

features.title: Fixtures for test data
features.description: Allows developers to manage mock API response data through fixture files rather than inline code.

features.title: Cypress Cloud test recording
features.description: Records test runs in the cloud with video, screenshots, and detailed failure analytics.

features.title: Parallelization across CI machines
features.description: Splits test suites across multiple machines in continuous integration to reduce total run time.

pros: Free and open source core framework under the MIT License
Strong developer experience with time-travel debugging and automatic waiting
Native component testing support for major front-end frameworks
Large, active community and extensive documentation
Cypress Cloud adds valuable CI parallelization and flake detection for teams
Remains an independent company, giving it a focused product roadmap
cons: Historically limited to Chromium-based and Firefox browsers with less mature WebKit/Safari support compared to some competitors
Playwright has grown faster in npm download share in recent years, indicating rising competitive pressure
Cypress Cloud paid tiers can become expensive at higher usage volumes
Runs inside the browser context, which creates some architectural limitations for certain multi-tab or multi-origin testing scenarios
Enterprise pricing is not transparently published and requires a sales conversation

faqs.question: Was Cypress acquired by Sauce Labs
faqs.answer: No. There is no verified evidence of a Sauce Labs acquisition of Cypress. Sauce Labs added official support for running Cypress tests on its cloud platform around February 2021, which is a technical integration partnership, not a corporate acquisition. Cypress.io, Inc. remains an independently operated company.

faqs.question: Is Cypress free to use
faqs.answer: Yes, the core Cypress Test Runner is free and open source under the MIT License. The optional Cypress Cloud service for test recording and parallelization has a free tier and paid plans.

faqs.question: When was Cypress founded
faqs.answer: Cypress.io, Inc. was founded in 2015 and is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.

faqs.question: How much does Cypress Cloud cost
faqs.answer: Cypress Cloud pricing spans a free Starter tier up to a Team plan around 67 USD per month and a Business plan around 267 USD per month, with custom Enterprise pricing above that.

faqs.question: Did John Deere acquire Cypress
faqs.answer: No. A blog post claiming Cypress was joining John Deere was published on April 1 (April Fools' Day) by a former Cypress team member and was explicitly satirical, not a real acquisition.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Cypress and Cypress Cloud
faqs.answer: Cypress is the free, open-source test runner used to write and execute tests locally or in CI. Cypress Cloud is the company's commercial SaaS product that adds recorded test history, parallelization, and analytics on top of the open-source runner.

faqs.question: Does Cypress support component testing
faqs.answer: Yes, Cypress supports native component testing for frameworks including React, Vue, and Angular, in addition to full end-to-end testing.

faqs.question: How does Cypress compare to Playwright
faqs.answer: Both are popular modern JavaScript testing frameworks; Cypress has strong developer experience and debugging tools, while Playwright has seen faster growth in weekly npm downloads and broader native cross-browser support in recent years.

use_cases.title: Automated end-to-end regression testing
use_cases.description: Engineering teams write Cypress tests to catch regressions across critical user flows before deployment.

use_cases.title: Component-level UI testing
use_cases.description: Teams test individual React, Vue, or Angular components in isolation using Cypress's native component testing support.

use_cases.title: CI/CD test parallelization
use_cases.description: QA and DevOps teams use Cypress Cloud to parallelize large test suites across CI machines and reduce pipeline run time.

features: Full end-to-end and component testing capability
No usage limits for local runs
Basic test recording and dashboard access
Tens of thousands of monthly test results
